issue.title: Go-replacement W: remove bigclaw-go e2e Python helpers tranche 2

commands: dirs=(); for p in README.md bigclaw-go/docs docs .github .husky .git/hooks; do [ -e "$p" ] && dirs+=("$p"); done; rg -n "bigclaw-go/scripts/e2e/.*\.py|scripts/e2e/.*\.py" "${dirs[@]}"
cd bigclaw-go && go run ./cmd/bigclawctl automation e2e run-task-smoke --help
cd bigclaw-go && go run ./cmd/bigclawctl automation e2e export-validation-bundle --help
cd bigclaw-go && go run ./cmd/bigclawctl automation e2e continuation-scorecard --help
cd bigclaw-go && go run ./cmd/bigclawctl automation e2e continuation-policy-gate --help

results.stale_reference_scan: no matches in existing README/docs/workflow/hooks/CI surfaces

blocker.summary: No additional in-repo implementation work remains for BIG-GO-1053.

notes: The tranche-2 e2e Python helper files were already absent in this checkout before the evidence commits were created, so the code migration itself is represented by origin/main at commit 004de016.
The final repo-wide Python count at the rebased main tip is 45 because BIG-GO-1057 also removed one Python entrypoint while this closeout was being rebased.
The follow-up rebased commits on main add the missing validation and closeout artifacts so the lane has the same in-repo reporting surface as adjacent Go-replacement issues.
